record.field: DocuSeal is an open-source electronic signature platform: a self-hostable DocuSign alternative to create, fill, and sign documents.

DocuSeal is an open-source electronic signature platform: a self-hostable DocuSign alternative to create, fill, and sign documents.

It provides a WYSIWYG builder for turning PDFs into fillable forms with multiple field types, then guides each signer through a step-by-step, mobile-friendly signing flow. Every document is sealed with an industry-standard digital PDF signature and an embedded audit trail, and the whole stack runs on your own infrastructure so sensitive contracts never leave your servers. It targets teams that want legally binding e-signatures without per-seat SaaS pricing or sending data to a third party.

Key features
  • WYSIWYG form builder with many field types (signature, date, checkbox, image, file, and more)
  • Step-by-step, mobile-optimized signing for multiple submitters
  • Standards-based digital PDF signatures with verification and an embedded audit trail
  • Automation via REST API and webhooks, plus automated SMTP email delivery
  • Flexible storage on local disk or private S3, Google Cloud Storage, or Azure
  • Compliance with eIDAS, ESIGN, UETA, HIPAA and 21 CFR Part 11; SSO/SAML and user roles available
Best for

Businesses, agencies and regulated organizations that need legally binding document signing on their own infrastructure, and developers integrating signing workflows into their apps through the API.

services: app: image: docuseal/docuseal:latest volumes: - ./docuseal:/data/docuseal depends_on: docu-postgres: condition: service_healthy restart: unless-stopped environment: - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://${POSTGRES_USER:-docuseal}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@docu-postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-docuseal} container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network docu-postgres: image: postgres:latest volumes: - ./docuseal_db:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB} restart: unless-stopped healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-docuseal} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-docuseal} interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_docu_postgres' networks: - internal docu-redis: image: redis:alpine restart: always volumes: - ./docuseal_redis_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_docu_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T docu-postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d docu-postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T docu-postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T docu-postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T docu-postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
